title: Blunting ends by 3’ overhang removal and 3’ recessed (5’ overhang) end fill-in using T4 DNA Polymerase (M0203)

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"38qli","text":"Please refer to the Safety Data Sheets (SDS) for health and environmental hazards.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Dissolve DNA in any 1 X, reaction buffer supplemented with 100 micromolar (µM), dNTPs . T4 DNA Polymerase can be used in NEBuffers 1.1 , 2.1 , and CutSmart® Buffer as well as NEBuffers 1 , 2 , and 4 and T4 DNA Ligase Reaction Buffer . Optimal activity is observed in NEBuffer 2.1 . BSA supplementation is recommended when using a buffer that does not already contain BSA.
Add 1 unit of T4 DNA Polymerase per microgram DNA.
Stop reaction by adding EDTA to a final concentration of 10 millimolar (mM) . See references 1, 2 for information on stopping reaction using EDTA and 75°C.

Heat for 00:20:00 at 75 °C . CAUTION : Elevated temperatures, excessive amounts of enzyme, failure to supplement with dNTPs or long reaction times will result in recessed ends due to the 3´→ 5´ exonuclease activity of the enzyme.
